message: bd-lff4p.5.11: run native PTY e2e suite with repro artifacts

@@ -1330,8 +1330,8 @@
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.1.9","title":"Test: proptest invariants for grid + parser (never panic; stable semantics)","description":"Goal
- Add property tests that make it safe to aggressively optimize and refactor.

Invariants (examples)
- Cursor always within bounds.
- Lines never exceed width in cell count.
- Parser accepts arbitrary bytes without panic.
- Applying a patch preserves semantic equality vs full recompute.

Acceptance Criteria
- Tests run in CI with fixed seeds + shrinkable counterexamples.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10","title":"FrankenTerm Remote: bidirectional protocol + PTY/websocket bridge","description":"Why
- Replacing xterm.js means we must support the full bidirectional terminal contract:
  - output parsing (VT/ANSI)
  - input encoding (keyboard/mouse -> bytes)
  - terminal replies to application queries (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - transport to a real PTY (local or remote)

Scope
- A minimal but correct PTY bridge (Rust) that can run locally and in production contexts.
- A secure websocket protocol for browser <-> PTY.
- End-to-end demos: connect FrankenTerm.WASM to a real shell and run scripted sessions.

Non-Negotiables
- Deterministic trace logging (JSONL) for every E2E session.
- Security: origin restrictions, explicit allowlists, no arbitrary command execution in prod.

Success Criteria
- Browser terminal can connect to a PTY bridge, run commands, and render accurately.
- Golden transcript tests prevent regressions.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:11:38.021410960Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","title":"Spec: FrankenTerm websocket protocol + threat model","description":"Goal
- Define the browser <-> PTY transport protocol and a production-safe threat model.

Must Cover
- Message types
  - input bytes / semantic events
  - resize
  - output chunks
  - terminal replies (DSR/DA/DEC)
  - clipboard (optional)
  - keepalive
- Versioning + schema
  - protocol version negotiation
  - JSON Schema / binary schema (explicit)
- Backpressure + flow control (prevent OOM)
  - bounded queues
  - coalescing policies (resize storms, mouse move floods)
  - fairness: interactive input must not be starved by output
- Security model
  - origin restrictions, auth, rate limits
  - allowed commands (if any), sandboxing
  - no implicit file access
  - explicit threat model + mitigations
- Capability identity
  - TERM/capability profile story for remote PTY sessions (ties to terminfo bead)
- Logging
  - every session emits JSONL with run_id/session_id, git_sha/build_id, sizes, wire counters, latency histograms

Acceptance Criteria
- Protocol is versioned and has a schema.
- E2E scripts can generate deterministic transcripts for golden checks.
- Threat model includes an explicit loss matrix (false allow vs false block) and a documented decision policy.
","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CobaltDog","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:49.528985806Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T19:54:49.528951862Z","close_reason":"Protocol spec complete: 761-line document covering message types, flow control, security, logging","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:06.729410386Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","title":"Implement: browser input encoder (DOM events -> terminal byte stream)","description":"Goal
- Encode browser keyboard/mouse events into a VT-compatible input byte stream suitable for a remote PTY.

Scope
- Key encoding: arrows, function keys, modifiers, paste, and common shortcuts.
- Mouse reporting sequences when enabled (SGR mouse).
- Respect feature toggles (bracketed paste, focus events, kitty keyboard where applicable).

Testing
- Unit tests for key/mouse mapping tables.
- Property tests for modifier state machines (no stuck mods).
- JSONL logs for E2E sessions include encoded byte lengths and event counts.
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.10","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.10.2","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:12:07.021846135Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-lff4p.10.3","title":"Implement: terminal reply engine (DSR/DA/DEC queries)","description":"Goal
- Support the bidirectional terminal contract by answering application queries (e.g. cursor position, device attributes).

Scope
- Identify and implement the minimal required set of queries for common CLI apps.
- Replies must be deterministic and capability-profiled.

Testing
- Conformance fixtures: query -> expected reply bytes.
- Differential tests against reference terminals where possible.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.4","title":"Implement: PTY bridge server (Rust) with websocket transport","description":"Goal
- Implement a Rust service that spawns a PTY and bridges I/O over websockets to FrankenTerm.WASM.

Scope
- Use portable-pty (already in tree) or a better alternative if justified.
- Enforce security constraints from the threat model.
- Expose structured telemetry: bytes in/out, latency, resize counts, exit status.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can run locally and drive interactive shells.
- Produces JSONL logs conforming to the shared schema.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.5","title":"E2E: scripted remote terminal sessions + golden transcripts","description":"Goal
- Add end-to-end scripts that connect the browser FrankenTerm to the PTY bridge and run deterministic scripted sessions.

Requirements
- Scripts must emit detailed JSONL (run_id, git_sha, sizes, wire counters, checksums).
- Golden transcripts are checksum-gated; failures produce actionable diffs.

Acceptance Criteria
- At least 5 high-signal scripts: resize storm, paste, unicode, OSC8 links, and long scrollback.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.10.6","title":"Perf: keystroke-to-photon latency budget + measurement harness","description":"Goal
- Define and enforce latency budgets for interactive remote sessions.

Metrics
- Keystroke -> rendered frame (p50/p95/p99).
- Round-trip websocket latency and jitter.
- Output throughput under load.

Acceptance Criteria
- Harness outputs machine-readable JSON and is gated against regressions.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","title":"Implement: IME/composition support (CJK input)","description":"Goal
- Provide high-quality text input beyond raw key events.

Acceptance Criteria
- Composition events do not corrupt terminal state.
- Works with common IMEs (macOS, Windows, Linux).
","notes":"Implemented IME composition normalization in frankenterm-web: added CompositionState/CompositionRewrite to canonicalize start/update/end/cancel streams, suppress key events during active composition, synthesize missing start events for malformed host sequences, and emit synthetic cancel on focus-loss to prevent stuck composition state. Wired wasm input ingestion through CompositionState rewrite path. Expanded composition phase parsing aliases (compositionstart/update/end/cancel + commit alias), and made composition data parsing strict-optional (type-checked when present). Added unit tests: composition_update_without_start_synthesizes_start, composition_drops_key_events_until_end, composition_focus_loss_emits_cancel_before_focus_event. Validation: cargo test -p frankenterm-web (pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --all-targets (pass), cargo clippy -p frankenterm-web --all-targets -- -D warnings (pass), cargo check -p frankenterm-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown (pass). Workspace-wide gates currently fail due unrelated concurrent changes in ftui-harness/ftui-demo-showcase/ftui-runtime.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BrightDune","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:13.809457202Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T20:22:13.809432977Z","close_reason":"Completed: IME composition normalization + tests + wasm compile validation","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.2.9","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.2.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:52:29.229709842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3","title":"ftui-web: compile ftui to WASM and run inside FrankenTerm","description":"Scope
- Compile ftui (core/render/layout/style/text/widgets/runtime + demo) to wasm and run inside FrankenTerm surface.
- Replace terminal polling with a step-based, JS-driven event loop.

Deliverables
- New `ftui-web` crate exposing a `WasmApp` API: new, input, resize, tick, render.
- Minimal web harness that runs ftui-demo-showcase fully client-side.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.1","title":"Implement: new crate ftui-web (WASM API surface + glue)","description":"Goal
- Add `crates/ftui-web` exposing a minimal embedding API for running ftui models in WASM.

API Sketch
- `WasmApp::new(opts)`
- `resize(cols, rows)`
- `input(event_bytes or semantic event)`
- `tick(dt)`
- `render() -> Patch or Frame`

Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo check -p ftui-web --target wasm32-unknown-unknown` succeeds.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.2","title":"Refactor: runtime backend abstraction (no direct Crossterm usage)","description":"Goal
- Introduce a backend abstraction so ftui-runtime can run on:
  - native tty backend (custom)
  - wasm backend (JS-driven)

Acceptance Criteria
- `Program` no longer owns a concrete `TerminalSession` tied to Crossterm.
- Backends provide: size, input events (or bytes), present output (or patches), and mode toggles.
- Old code paths removed after migration (no lingering shims).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.3.4","title":"Implement: WASM step-based Program runner (no blocking poll, no threads)","description":"Goal
- Implement a deterministic, JS-driven runtime loop for WASM.

Requirements
- Inputs are delivered from JS (keyboard/mouse/resize/ticks).
- Rendering produces either ftui-render Buffer diffs or FrankenTerm patches.
- No `std::thread` assumptions.

Acceptance Criteria
- Can drive an ftui Model with: init -> input -> view -> present cycles.
- Deterministic record/replay possible.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.4.5","title":"Integrate: swap ftui runtime to new backend (native path)","description":"Goal
- Wire the new native backend into `ftui-runtime` and remove all direct Crossterm coupling from the runtime hot path.

Non-Negotiable Compatibility
- Behavior must match current demo semantics (input, resize, mouse, paste, focus, kitty keyboard, alt-screen/inline rules).
- One-writer rule and terminal cleanup semantics must remain correct under normal exit and panic/unwind.

Testing + Logging
- Run the existing PTY E2E suite (`tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` or curated subsets) against the new backend.
- E2E logs must validate against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and include backend identity + capability profile.
- Add/extend tests as needed to cover any behavior differences discovered during migration.

Acceptance Criteria
- `ftui-demo-showcase` runs on the new backend with identical user-visible behavior.
- E2E suite is green and produces detailed JSONL logs usable for regression triage.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5","title":"FrankenTerm and ftui-web QA: golden traces, fuzz, extreme optimization","description":"Scope
- Correctness and performance engineering for the entire FrankenTerm + ftui-web stack (core/web/native/remote).

Deliverables
- Golden systems
  - Versioned trace format + replay engine + checksum gates
  - Renderer-level goldens (frame/pixel hashes) for web
- Test stack
  - Proptest invariants + fuzz targets for parsers/state machines
  - Native PTY E2E scripts with detailed JSONL logging
  - Web/remote E2E scripts with deterministic traces + JSONL
- CI + Perf gates
  - wasm build checks
  - time-bounded fuzz/proptest smoke
  - trace replay gates
  - benchmark/perf regression thresholds (with optional statistical guards)

Operating Principle
- Every optimization is single-lever, measured, and backed by isomorphism proof + golden verification.
","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:36.166705937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:16:59.671450506Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:49:36.166705937Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","title":"Design: golden trace format for terminal sessions (native + web)","description":"Goal
- Define a stable, versioned trace format we can use to reproduce bugs and gate regressions across core/web/native/remote.

Format Requirements
- Inputs
  - output byte streams (VT/ANSI) and/or patches
  - input events (keyboard/mouse/touch/IME)
  - resize events
  - tick/time deltas (explicitly injected)
- Checkpoints
  - frame hashes and/or patch hashes
  - optional grid snapshots for debugging
- Metadata
  - run_id, git_sha/build_id, seed
  - size, DPR (web), terminal profile/capabilities, TERM identity (remote)
  - perf counters: timing histograms, patch sizes, bytes in/out

Design Constraints
- Forward-migratable schema (versioned, with strict validation).
- Reducible/minimizable: traces must be amenable to automatic minimization (delta debugging).

Acceptance Criteria
- Trace format is versioned and forward-migratable.
- Can be produced by native E2E scripts, web harnesses, and remote PTY sessions.
- Validator produces actionable errors (line + field context).
","notes":"Drafted canonical trace spec at docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md (JSONL bundle layout, record types, hashing/normalization, payload sidecars, CI validation + minimization strategy).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkMill","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703034522Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:45:08.703008854Z","close_reason":"Completed: docs/spec/frankenterm-golden-trace-format.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T08:55:10.780434801Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","title":"Alien Artifact: statistical perf regression detection (e-values/Bayes)","description":"Goal
- Reduce perf-gate false positives while catching real regressions using principled statistics.

Alien Artifact Techniques
- Anytime-valid hypothesis testing via e-values/e-processes (optional stopping safe).
- Bayesian performance model (posterior over regression magnitude) + Bayes factors for evidence strength.
- Explicit expected-loss decision rule:
  - define loss matrix for false positive (wasted engineer time) vs false negative (shipping regression)
  - choose thresholds/policies that minimize expected loss under measured noise.

Deliverables
- A confidence report and evidence ledger per perf gate:
  - p-value alternatives (e-values), posterior summaries, and Bayes factors
  - dataset summary (runs, variance, hardware)
  - decision + rationale
- Clear override/rollback policy when noise dominates.

Acceptance Criteria
- Perf gates become robust: materially fewer flaky failures without letting real regressions through.
- Outputs are machine-readable and human-auditable.
","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:25:06.635143696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.10","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:13:19.554603628Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

","notes":"Implemented in `.github/workflows/ci.yml` (`e2e-pty` job):
- PTY step now runs `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` explicitly (native PTY suite entrypoint).
- Kept strict JSONL validation against `tests/e2e/lib/e2e_jsonl_schema.json` and hash registry.
- Added `Build PTY failure repro bundle` step (`if: always()`) that emits:
  - `minimal_repros.md` (failed test -> minimal repro script path)
  - `*_excerpt.log` snippets around failure markers
  - `*_pty_head.hex` first 512 bytes for failed PTY captures
  - copied per-run `summary.json`
- Added always-on JSONL artifact upload (`e2e-pty-jsonl-${{ matrix.os }}`) and repro-bundle upload (`e2e-pty-failure-bundle-${{ matrix.os }}`).

Validation:
- YAML parse check passed via Ruby `YAML.load_file`.
- Shell syntax check passed for `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh` and `scripts/e2e_test.sh`.
- Local sanity run: `tests/e2e/scripts/run_all.sh --quick` produced summary + JSONL + logs under `/tmp/ftui_e2e_ci_sanity_1`.
- Repro-bundle logic dry-run produced `minimal_repros.md`, excerpts, and PTY hex artifacts; sample mapping resolved `inline_basic` -> `tests/e2e/scripts/test_inline.sh`.

Repo-wide gate context during this session:
- `cargo check --all-targets`: passed.
- `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`: fails in unrelated `frankenterm-web` borrow-check changes (`E0499` in `crates/frankenterm-web/src/glyph_atlas.rs`).

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.12","title":"CI: run golden trace replay gates (core + web + remote)","description":"Goal
- Turn the golden corpus into a hard regression gate.

Scope
- Replay traces through:
  - frankenterm-core (parser/grid)
  - ftui-web runner (state machine + rendering)
  - remote bridge loop (where feasible)

Acceptance Criteria
- CI fails on checksum mismatches with actionable diffs (frame/event indices).
- Time-bounded execution for PRs; expanded coverage on nightly.

 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","title":"Alien Artifact: failing-trace minimizer (delta debugging + VOI)","description":"Goal
- When a golden trace fails (checksum mismatch), automatically minimize it to the smallest repro trace that still fails.

Why (User Value)
- Makes regressions debuggable in minutes: a minimized trace is actionable and can be attached to issues/PRs.

Techniques (Alien Artifact)
- Start with classic delta debugging (ddmin) on the event stream.
- Upgrade with Value-of-Information (VOI) heuristics to pick the next reduction step that maximizes expected information gain per replay cost.
- Maintain an evidence ledger: each reduction attempt logs (candidate size, replay time, pass/fail, inferred minimality bounds).

Deliverables
- CLI/API to minimize a trace given a predicate ("replay mismatches").
- Produces a new trace file + a minimization report (JSON/JSONL).

Acceptance Criteria
- On a representative failing trace, produces >=10x size reduction while preserving the failure.
- Deterministic: repeated minimization yields identical minimized trace on same build.
- Integrates into CI failure artifacts (minimized trace attached when feasible/time-bounded).
","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-lff4p.5.13","depends_on_id":"bd-lff4p.5.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:24:10.918614366Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-lff4p.5.14","title":"Alien Artifact: exhaustive small-state model checking for terminal invariants","description":"Goal
- Go beyond randomized property tests: exhaustively explore small terminal states to prove invariants hold under all short sequences of operations.

Approach
- Enumerate state space for tiny grids (e.g. 2x2, 3x3) and bounded scrollback.
- Enumerate short operation sequences (parser ops, resize ops, selection ops) and verify invariants after each step.
- Use pruning + canonicalization to keep exploration tractable.

Deliverables
- A deterministic model-check runner producing a coverage report (state counts, transition counts, found counterexamples).
- Any counterexample becomes a regression fixture in the conformance corpus and/or trace corpus.

Acceptance Criteria
- Runs time-bounded in CI (small sizes) and extended locally (bigger bounds).
- Finds at least one non-trivial bug during development (expectation: state machines are hard).

@@ -558,6 +560,82 @@ jobs:
               --strict
           done
 
+      - name: Build PTY failure repro bundle
+        if: always()
+        run: |
+          set -euo pipefail
+          shopt -s nullglob
+
+          bundle_root="/tmp/ftui_e2e_failure_bundle_${{ matrix.os }}"
+          mkdir -p "$bundle_root"
+
+          summary_files=(/tmp/ftui_e2e_*/results/summary.json)
+          if (( ${#summary_files[@]} == 0 )); then
+            echo "No PTY E2E summary files were generated." > "$bundle_root/README.txt"
+            exit 0
+          fi
+
+          {
+            echo "# PTY E2E Minimal Repros"
+            echo
+            echo "Generated: $(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")"
+            echo
+            echo "Each failed test maps to its minimal repro script in \`tests/e2e/scripts/\`."
+            echo
+          } > "$bundle_root/minimal_repros.md"
+
+          for summary in "${summary_files[@]}"; do
+            run_dir="$(dirname "$(dirname "$summary")")"
+            run_name="$(basename "$run_dir")"
+            cp "$summary" "$bundle_root/${run_name}_summary.json"
+
+            failed_count="$(jq -r '.failed // 0' "$summary" 2>/dev/null || echo 0)"
+            if [[ "$failed_count" == "0" ]]; then
+              continue
+            fi
+
+            jq -r '.tests[] | select(.status=="failed") | .name' "$summary" > "$bundle_root/${run_name}_failed_tests.txt"
+
+            while IFS= read -r test_name; do
+              [[ -n "$test_name" ]] || continue
+
+              suite_name="${test_name%%_*}"
+              script_path="tests/e2e/scripts/test_${suite_name}.sh"
+              case_script="tests/e2e/scripts/test_${test_name}.sh"
+              if [[ ! -f "$script_path" && -f "$case_script" ]]; then
+                script_path="$case_script"
+              fi
+              if [[ ! -f "$script_path" ]]; then
+                candidate="$(rg --files tests/e2e/scripts | rg "/test_(${suite_name}|${test_name})\\.sh$" | head -n1 || true)"
+                if [[ -n "$candidate" ]]; then
+                  script_path="$candidate"
+                else
+                  script_path="(script not found)"
+                fi
+              fi
+
+              echo "- \`${test_name}\`: \`${script_path}\`" >> "$bundle_root/minimal_repros.md"
+
+              log_file="$run_dir/e2e.log"
+              excerpt_file="$bundle_root/${run_name}_${test_name}_excerpt.log"
+              if [[ -f "$log_file" ]]; then
+                awk -v needle="$test_name" '
+                  index($0, needle) {
+                    start = NR - 20;
+                    if (start < 1) start = 1;
+                    end = NR + 40;
+                  }
+                  NR >= start && NR <= end { print }
+                ' "$log_file" > "$excerpt_file" || true
+              fi
+
+              pty_file="$run_dir/${test_name}.pty"
+              if [[ -f "$pty_file" ]]; then
+                xxd -l 512 "$pty_file" > "$bundle_root/${run_name}_${test_name}_pty_head.hex" || true
+              fi
+            done < "$bundle_root/${run_name}_failed_tests.txt"
+          done
+
       - name: Upload E2E logs on failure
         if: failure()
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
@@ -566,6 +644,24 @@ jobs:
           path: /tmp/ftui_e2e_*/
           retention-days: 7
 
+      - name: Upload PTY E2E JSONL logs
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: e2e-pty-jsonl-${{ matrix.os }}
+          path: /tmp/ftui_e2e_*/**/*.jsonl
+          retention-days: 7
+          if-no-files-found: error
+
+      - name: Upload PTY failure repro bundle
+        if: always()
+        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
+        with:
+          name: e2e-pty-failure-bundle-${{ matrix.os }}
+          path: /tmp/ftui_e2e_failure_bundle_${{ matrix.os }}/
+          retention-days: 7
+          if-no-files-found: ignore
+
       - name: Upload test summary
         if: always()
         uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
